name: Traps
description: Rules for trap design, usage, mechanics, and 8 example traps spanning levels 1-20

design_philosophy.overview: Traps should be used sparingly, lest they lose their charm
design_philosophy.key_principle: A hidden pit can be a fun surprise, but too many traps in an adventure can lead players to become overly cautious, which slows down the game
best_practices: The best traps are fleeting distractions that skilled characters can overcome in a short amount of time
Deadly puzzles that require quick thinking and teamwork to overcome are engaging
Traps that are undetectable and inescapable are rarely fun
Caution: A trap that is a nuisance at one level range could be deadly to characters of a lower level range

parts_of_a_trap.title: Parts of a Trap
parts_of_a_trap.description: The description of a trap includes the following parts after the trap's name

severity_and_levels.name: Severity and Levels
severity_and_levels.description: A trap is designated as a nuisance or as deadly for characters of certain levels

definitions.nuisance_trap: Unlikely to kill or seriously harm characters of the indicated levels
definitions.deadly_trap: Can grievously damage characters of the indicated levels

trigger.name: Trigger
trigger.description: Traps are often set to go off when a creature enters an area or touches an object
examples: Using the wrong key in a lock

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long the trap's effects last
types: Duration expressed in rounds, minutes, or hours
Lasts until the trap is destroyed or dispelled

example_traps.title: Example Traps
example_traps.description: Traps are presented in alphabetical order

collapsing_roof.name: Collapsing Roof
collapsing_roof.trigger: A creature crosses a trip wire
collapsing_roof.description: This trap uses a trip wire to collapse an unstable section of ceiling. The trip wire is 3 inches off the ground and stretches between two weak supports that topple when the trip wire is pulled.

mechanics.effect: The first creature that crosses the trip wire causes the supports to topple and the unstable section of ceiling to collapse
mechanics.targets: Each creature beneath the unstable section of ceiling
mechanics.damage: 11 (2d10) Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one
mechanics.additional: Rubble from the collapse turns the trapped area into Difficult Terrain

detect.detects: The trip wire and the unstable section of ceiling

detect_and_disarm.disarm: Once detected, the trip wire can be easily cut or avoided (no ability check required)

falling_net.name: Falling Net
falling_net.trigger: A creature crosses a trip wire
falling_net.description: A falling net trap uses a trip wire to release a weighted, 10-foot-square Net suspended from the ceiling. The trip wire is 3 inches off the ground and stretches between two columns or trees.

mechanics.effect: The first creature that crosses the trip wire causes the Net to fall on it
mechanics.failure: Has the Restrained condition until it escapes
mechanics.automatic_success: The target succeeds automatically if it's Huge or larger

escape.effect: Freeing itself or another creature within its reach from the Net on a successful check

detect.detects: The trip wire and the suspended Net

detect_and_disarm.disarm: Once detected, the trip wire can be easily cut or avoided (no ability check required)

falling_net.destroy_net: Reducing the Net to 0 Hit Points frees any creature trapped in it

set_trap.requirement: Thieves' Tools and all the trap's components (including a Net)

higher_levels.scaling: Increasing the weight of the Net increases the save DC and the DC of the Strength (Athletics) check

fire_casting_statue.name: Fire-Casting Statue
fire_casting_statue.trigger: A creature moves onto a pressure plate
fire_casting_statue.description: When a creature moves onto this trap's pressure plate for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, a nearby statue exhales a 15-foot Cone of magical flame. The statue can look like anything, such as a dragon or a wizard.

mechanics.damage: 11 (2d10) Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one

detect_and_disarm.detect_magic: A Detect Magic spell reveals an aura of Evocation magic around the statue

find_glyph.detects: A tiny glyph on the statue

deface_glyph.effect: Defacing the glyph disarms the trap

pressure_plate.method: Examine the section of floor that forms the pressure plate
pressure_plate.disarm: Wedging an Iron Spike or similar object under the pressure plate prevents the trap from triggering

hidden_pit.name: Hidden Pit
hidden_pit.trigger: A creature moves onto the pit's lid
hidden_pit.description: This 10-foot-deep pit has a hinged lid constructed from material identical to the surrounding floor. When a creature moves onto the lid, it swings open like a trapdoor, causing the creature to fall into the pit. The lid remains open thereafter.

disarm_methods: Wedge an Iron Spike or similar object between the pit's lid and the surrounding floor to prevent the lid from opening
Hold the cover shut using Arcane Lock or similar magic

hidden_pit.escape: A creature needs a Climb Speed, climbing gear, or magic such as Spider Climb to scale the pit's smooth walls. (You can make the pit easier to escape by adding cracks in the walls big enough to serve as handholds and footholds.)

poisoned_darts.name: Poisoned Darts
poisoned_darts.trigger: A creature moves onto a pressure plate
poisoned_darts.description: When a creature moves onto this trap's pressure plate for the first time on a turn, poisoned darts shoot from tubes embedded in the surrounding walls. The holes that house these tubes are obscured by dust and cobwebs, or skillfully hidden amid bas-reliefs, murals, or frescoes.

mechanics.targets: Each creature in the darts' path
mechanics.effect: Be struck by 1d3 darts, taking 3 (1d6) Poison damage per dart on a failed save

find_holes.disarm: Plugging all the holes with wax, cloth, or detritus prevents the darts from firing

find_pressure_plate.disarm: Wedging an Iron Spike or similar object under the pressure plate prevents the trap from triggering

poisoned_needle.name: Poisoned Needle
poisoned_needle.trigger: A creature opens the trap's lock improperly or fails to disarm the trap
poisoned_needle.description: A poisoned needle is hidden in a lock. When a creature opens the lock with any object other than the proper key, the needle springs out and stabs the creature.

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour

mechanics.success: Takes half as much damage only

poisoned_needle.avoid: The trap doesn't trigger if the lock is opened using a Knock spell or similar magic

disarm.effect: On a failed check, the creature triggers the trap

rolling_stone.name: Rolling Stone
rolling_stone.trigger: A creature moves onto a pressure plate
rolling_stone.description: When a creature moves onto a hidden pressure plate, a 5-foot-radius orb of solid stone is released from a secret compartment and begins to roll.

mechanics.initiative: The stone and all creatures nearby roll Initiative; the stone gets a +8 bonus on its Initiative roll
mechanics.movement: On its turn, the stone moves 60 feet in one direction, changing course if redirected by an obstacle
mechanics.space: The stone can move through creatures' spaces, and creatures can move through the stone's space, treating it as Difficult Terrain
mechanics.collision: Whenever the stone enters a creature's space for the first time on a turn or a creature enters the stone's space while the stone is rolling
mechanics.effect: On a failed save, take 55 (10d10) Bludgeoning damage and have the Prone condition
mechanics.stopping: The stone stops when it hits a wall or similar barrier. It can't go around corners, but creative dungeon builders incorporate curving turns into nearby passages that allow the stone to keep moving

detect_and_disarm.disarm: Wedging an Iron Spike or similar object under the pressure plate prevents the trap from triggering

slow_stone.effect: If successful, the distance the stone moves on its turn is reduced by 15 feet
slow_stone.stopping: If that distance drops to 0, it stops moving and is no longer a threat

spiked_pit.name: Spiked Pit
spiked_pit.trigger: A creature moves onto the pit's lid
spiked_pit.description: A 10-foot-deep pit has a hinged lid constructed from material identical to the surrounding floor. When a creature moves onto the lid, it swings open like a trapdoor, causing the creature to fall into the pit, which has sharpened wooden or metal spikes at the bottom. The lid remains open thereafter.

mechanics.damage: 3 (1d6) Bludgeoning damage from the fall plus 9 (2d8) Piercing damage from the spikes

disarm_methods: Wedge an Iron Spike or similar object between the pit's lid and the surrounding floor to prevent the lid from opening
Hold the cover shut using an Arcane Lock spell or similar magic

spiked_pit.escape: A creature needs a Climb Speed, climbing gear, or magic such as a Spider Climb spell to scale the pit's smooth walls. You can make the pit easier to escape by adding cracks in the walls big enough to serve as handholds and footholds.

contains: Trap design philosophy and best practices
Parts of a trap (Severity, Trigger, Duration)
Collapsing Roof (deadly, trip wire, terrain hazard)
Fire-Casting Statue (deadly, cone damage, multiple detection methods)
Hidden Pit (nuisance, fall damage, escape mechanics)
Poisoned Darts (deadly, multiple targets, damage per dart)
Rolling Stone (deadly/nuisance by level, initiative, collision mechanics)
Spiked Pit (deadly, fall + spike damage, scaling by level)
Scaling tables for higher character levels (5-10, 11-16, 17-20)
